alerts.title: Iran launches one-way attack drones at Strait of Hormuz; US intercepts all
alerts.summary: US Central Command reported Iran launched multiple one-way attack drones targeting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz on June 13. All drones were downed by US forces; international trade corridor remains open (ME Spectator, ~35K views). This marks sustained Iranian escalation despite ceasefire framework discussions and represents direct threat to global commerce.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment spikes to 54% amid Iran tensions and Trump messaging
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US negative sentiment reached 54% (from baseline ~48%), with 'trump' (103 mentions), 'iran' (17 mentions), and 'deal' (15 mentions) dominating word cloud on June 13. Coincides with Iran drone attacks and contradictory US-Iran diplomatic signaling, indicating public anxiety over ceasefire stability and escalation risk.

alerts.title: Three US carriers and amphibious task force maintain maximum posture in Middle East
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) remain deployed with destroyer and submarine support (USS Florida in Persian Gulf, USS Bataan in Red Sea). Sustained high-end force composition indicates no de-escalation despite diplomatic claims (assets, 54 articles analyzed).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Red Sea, Arabian Sea

alerts.title: Ukraine deep-strike drone campaign intensifies; Rybar reports 60% decoy tactics
alerts.summary: Rybar posted high-engagement analysis (144K views, June 12) on Ukrainian BLA drone operations across Russian territory, noting shift to precision tactics by mid-2026. New development: 60% of May drones launched were decoys to deplete Russian air defense—strategic evolution beyond volume saturation (Rybar, tagged 'strike' keyword).

alerts.title: Brent crude holds at $87.33 amid ceasefire uncertainty; Iranian rial remains volatile
alerts.summary: Global metrics show Brent crude at $87.33 (stable) and Iranian rial free-market rate at 1,375,255 (high volatility). Threat level registered at 7/10 (HIGH-ELEVATED). Despite tactical ceasefire claims, economic indicators reflect sustained uncertainty over Iran's willingness to honor commitments and risk of renewed escalation.

analysis_note: Iran is conducting measured but repeated escalations (drone attacks on Hormuz shipping) while ostensibly negotiating with the US; Trump administration maintains maximum military posture while pursuing diplomatic off-ramps; Ukraine continues tactical innovation in deep-strike campaigns; regional stability remains fragile with multiple potential flashpoints active.
